baseline.subject: Add Mira Core Phase 38 runtime readiness refresh validation
baseline.baseline_boundary: Phases 1-38 are committed as local read-only, proposal-only, or validation-only Mira Core artifacts. No UI implementation, browser/window/capture access, runtime, server, listener, route, network, database, store, file migration, queue, lease, auth, key, secret, local execution, shell, PTY, control execution, reporting sink, send, deploy, trade, output-file, durable memory/profile commit, export, delete, restore, or raw-content storage behavior exists at this baseline.

purpose: Define a validation-only disabled local dry-run readiness gap assessment after committed Phase38 at 85c78ab.
Pin phase inventory, schema registry, and CLI registry coverage through Phases 1-38.
Record that the Phase38 Tier0 recommendation phase39-runtime-readiness-refresh-through-phase37-validator is satisfied by 85c78ab and must not be repeated as open work.
Select Phase38 Tier1 recommendation phase39-disabled-local-dry-run-readiness-gap-contract as fixture-only work.
Preserve Phase35, Phase36, Phase37, and Phase38 as current validation truth while keeping stale Phase13, Phase23, and Phase31 truth intact.
Make the remaining disabled local dry-run readiness gaps visible as unsatisfied, blocking, non-authorizing gates.

Keep runtimeStarted=false, runnerExecuted=false, runtimeAvailable=false, serverCanExecuteLocal=false, serverCanProveModelProcessing=false, direct Builder/Oracle server targets blocked, and proof boundaries false.
nonGoals: No browser, window, screenshot, OCR, or capture access.
No runtime implementation or runtime start.
No kill-switch wiring, rollback execution, reporting sink, store, queue, lease, or output-file behavior.
No server, listener, route, HTTP, WebSocket, or network behavior.
No database, store, file migration, auth, token, session, signing, key, encryption, KMS, env secret, or secret material behavior.
No local execution, shell, PTY, send, deploy, trade, financial action, export, delete, restore, memory/profile commit, or raw-content storage behavior.

No claim that Phase38 committed validation, readiness-gap labels, disabled control labels, reporting summaries, operator surfaces, socket connection, delivery acceptance, or this fixture proves runtime availability, model processing, bridge green, local-arm execution, control execution, or runner execution.

sourceRefsExpected.purpose: disabled local dry-run implementation contract-only boundary source
ORACLE #131 runtime status milestone refresh source
ORACLE #148/#149 runtime readiness refresh review source

gapMapExpected.evidence_required: A future code path reads an explicit disabled-by-default local dev runtime mode flag without enabling runtime.
A future kill switch is wired into the dry-run entry path, defaults fail-closed, and is test-covered.
A future dry-run request path is deterministic, preview-only, and covered without executing local work.
A future binding is in-process or loopback-only and proves no public/remote listener behavior.
A future telemetry/audit sink is redacted, separately gated, and does not leak raw payloads.
Future storage path, env config, key refs, and auth refs are validated by reference only with no secret read.
A future operator-visible local status surface shows disabled/local/dry-run status without UI proof or browser/window access.
A future rollback/disable exercise is idempotent, test-covered, non-destructive, and cannot resurrect blocked state.
Future proof demonstrates dry-run request to status response without side effects, queue/lease/store/output writes, or proof overclaims.

staticValidationRules.description: Readiness gap baseline_commit must equal 85c78ab.
Phase38 readiness refresh must be marked current and committed at 85c78ab.
Phase35 runtime next-action truth must remain current.
Phase36 operator UI surface truth must remain current.
Phase37 control/reporting reconciliation truth must remain current.
Phase inventory must cover exactly committed Phases 1-38.
Schema registry must cover exactly 38 phases.
CLI registry must cover exactly 38 phases.
Commit chain must include Phase 1-13 package plus Phase 14-38 commits in order and end at 85c78ab.
Phase38 Tier1 disabled local dry-run readiness gap recommendation must be selected as contract-only work.
Phase38 Tier0 readiness refresh validator recommendation must be satisfied by 85c78ab and not reopened.
Phase34 prior recommendations remain satisfied and closed.
Prior stale Phase13 readiness truth must be preserved.
Prior stale Phase23 readiness truth must be preserved.
Phase31 runtime milestone refresh must remain stale/superseded by Phase34.
ORACLE #115, #123, #127, #131, #134, #137, #141, #148, and #149 closures must be carried.
Source refs must include Phase29/30/32/33/34/35/36/37/38 artifacts.
Recent Phase35/36/37/38 fixture/module/CLI/test paths must be named for future reference checks.
Readiness gap matrix must include exactly the nine disabled local dry-run gaps.
Each readiness gap remains unsatisfied, blocks runtime/dry-run now, and is non-authorizing.
Disabled local dry-run boundary must remain unavailable now, disabled-by-default, dev-only, and local/in-process or loopback only.
Readiness gap assessment must not authorize runtime, dry-run runner execution, local arms, queue, lease, store, reporting sink, or output files.
runtimeStarted, runnerExecuted, runtimeAvailable, serverCanExecuteLocal, serverCanProveModelProcessing, and direct Builder/Oracle targets must stay false.
Socket, delivery acceptance, Phase38 commit, control report, operator surface, and readiness gap cannot become proof.
All no-side-effect truth fields must remain true.
Phase39 Oracle contract must not include module, CLI, test, runtime, UI, reporting sink, or output-file behavior.
No raw/private/terminal/screenshot/browser/secret/customer-private content may be included.
Next recommendations must be Tier0/Tier1 only and non-authorizing.
Recommendations and narrative fields must reject customer-send/deploy/trade/file-write/store-write/local-execution/control-execution/reporting-sink drift.
Validation report must include required literal checks and bind their ok values to recomputed fixture-required literals.
Validation report static, acceptance, tamper, required literal, and referenced path results must match fixture IDs/counts and expected failures.
Equivalent manifest inputs produce stable idempotency; phase registry, readiness gap, source recommendation, closure, baseline, or commit changes alter idempotency.

nextRecommendationExpectedCandidates.action: implement validation-only disabled local dry-run readiness gap report against this fixture
nextRecommendationExpectedCandidates.why_safe: data-only gap validation for committed Phases 1-38; no module beyond validator/report, no UI rendering, browser/window/capture, runtime, network, store, queue, lease, execution, reporting sink, send, deploy, trade, or output-file behavior

nextRecommendationExpectedCandidates.action: create fixture-only runtime mode and kill-switch reference contract after Phase39 validation is committed
nextRecommendationExpectedCandidates.why_safe: contract-only reference-boundary work for two readiness gaps that keeps runtimeStarted=false, runnerExecuted=false, runtimeAvailable=false, serverCanExecuteLocal=false, queue/lease/store/reporting-sink/output-file effects blocked, and proof boundaries false

summaryCriteria: Fixture remains the only Phase39 file created by Oracle.
Phase inventory, schema registry, and CLI registry requirements cover exactly Phases 1-38.
Commit chain contains Phase 1-13 package plus Phase 14-38 scoped commits and ends at 85c78ab.
Source recommendation phase39-disabled-local-dry-run-readiness-gap-contract is selected as Tier1 fixture-only work.
Phase38 Tier0 recommendation phase39-runtime-readiness-refresh-through-phase37-validator is satisfied by 85c78ab and not reopened.
Phase35, Phase36, Phase37, and Phase38 are current; stale Phase13, Phase23, and Phase31 truth is preserved.
Phase34 prior recommendations remain satisfied and not reopened.
ORACLE #115, ORACLE #123, ORACLE #127, ORACLE #131, ORACLE #134, ORACLE #137, ORACLE #141, ORACLE #148, and ORACLE #149 closures are explicit.
Source refs include Phase29, Phase30, Phase32, Phase33, Phase34, Phase35, Phase36, Phase37, and Phase38 artifacts.
Readiness gap matrix includes exactly nine disabled local dry-run gaps, all unsatisfied, blocking runtime/dry-run now, and non-authorizing.

Dry-run boundary remains unavailable now, disabled-by-default, dev-only, local/in-process or loopback only, with no runtime mode implementation, kill-switch wiring, tested dry-run path, queue/lease, store writes, reporting sink, or output files.

Capability and proof boundaries keep runtimeStarted=false, runnerExecuted=false, runtimeAvailable=false, serverCanExecuteLocal=false, serverCanProveModelProcessing=false, direct Builder/Oracle targets blocked, and proof boundaries false.

All side-effect truth fields preserve no module/CLI/test implementation, UI/runtime/server/listener/network/db/store/file migration/queue/lease/auth/key/secret/local execution/shell/PTY/browser/window/capture/control execution/reporting sink/send/deploy/trade/output-file behavior.

Next recommendations are Tier0/Tier1 only, non-authorizing, and do not recommend live UI, browser/window/capture access, live runtime, customer send, deploy, trade, output files, store writes, queue/lease creation, control execution, reporting sink, or local execution.
